item.text: I’ll verify the few code-shape assumptions behind the R2 plan before giving the short verdict, especially path derivation and `_cmd_auto_execute` ordering.

item.aggregated_output:
1	"""v0.8.1 dispatch shim. Bridges orchestrator (Python) to subagent
     2	invocation. Resolution order: env var -> capability binding ->
     3	RuntimeError.
     4	
     5	The shim is intentionally thin - the capability binding (Step 22.4)
     6	points at the real Claude CLI invocation; tests substitute via the
     7	``FLOW_SUBAGENT_DISPATCH_CMD`` env var (see test_subagent_dispatch_shim.py).
     8	
     9	Wire-up (S-class defended):
    10	    scripts/flow_orchestrator.py::_invoke_subagent_dispatch ->
    11	        importlib.import_module("flow_subagent_dispatch") ->
    12	            .invoke(ctx, subagent_env=subagent_env)
    13	
    14	The orchestrator passes the parent PID env (T21 / S5) via
    15	``subagent_env``; this shim merges it into the subprocess environment so
    16	the spawned subagent inherits ``FLOW_AUTONOMY_PARENT_PID`` and the
    17	nested-autonomy mechanical guard fires correctly.
    18	
    19	R-class hardening: ``slug`` and ``task_id`` are interpolated into a
    20	``shell=True`` command string. Even though the env var/capability
    21	template is operator-controlled (so this is operator -> operator, not
    22	user -> operator), shell metacharacters in identifiers would let a
    23	malformed manifest leak into a compound command. We validate the
    24	character set up front and raise ``ValueError`` rather than risk
    25	``rm -rf $HOME``-style surprises if a future contract spec relaxes the
    26	slug regex.
    27	
    28	Template placeholders (codex round-2 P2 — preserve quoted templates):
    29	    {slug}             — task slug, validated [A-Za-z0-9._+\\-]
    30	    {task_id}          — task id, validated [A-Za-z0-9._+\\-]
    31	    {worktree}         — RAW worktree path. Backward-compatible with
    32	                         operator templates that already wrap the
    33	                         placeholder in shell quotes (e.g.
    34	                         ``--worktree "{worktree}"``). Use this when
    35	                         your template controls quoting itself.
    36	    {worktree_quoted}  — shlex.quote()-wrapped worktree path. RECOMMENDED
    37	                         default for shell=True templates without their
    38	                         own quoting (e.g.
    39	                         ``--worktree {worktree_quoted}``).
    40	
    41	    History: round-1 silently swapped {worktree} -> shlex.quote(...),
    42	    which broke any template that already used outer quotes — the inner
    43	    single quotes injected by shlex.quote() were preserved literally and
    44	    the subagent received ``'/path with space'`` (with quote chars). The
    45	    fix splits raw vs quoted into two named placeholders so existing
    46	    templates keep working and the safe form is opt-in.
    47	"""
    48	from __future__ import annotations
    49	
    50	import json
    51	import os
    52	import re
    53	import shlex
    54	import subprocess
    55	import sys
    56	from pathlib import Path
    57	from typing import Any
    58	
    59	
    60	ENV_VAR = "FLOW_SUBAGENT_DISPATCH_CMD"
    61	# G/S-class (codex round-1 F3): resolve relative to THIS module's install
    62	# location, NOT relative to cwd. Production callers (`flow orchestrator
    63	# --auto-execute`) chdir into a user project root that has only ``.flow/``
    64	# under it — the framework's own ``claude/capabilities/defaults.json``
    65	# lives next to ``scripts/`` in the framework install. Using cwd-relative
    66	# resolution made the capability fallback unreachable in production and
    67	# turned every dispatch into the explicit RuntimeError, even when the
    68	# (now-removed) dispatch_cmd default was in place.
    69	CAPABILITY_FILE = (
    70	    Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    71	    / "claude" / "capabilities" / "defaults.json"
    72	)
    73	
    74	# R-class: only allow chars that cannot terminate a shell token. Mirror the
    75	# slug regex used by flow_contract / flow_doctor (alnum + dot + underscore +
    76	# plus + dash). Empty strings are rejected because an empty {task_id}
    77	# substitution would silently collapse to ``-task `` style fragments which
    78	# are semantically meaningless.
    79	_IDENT_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9._+\-]+$")
    80	
    81	
    82	def _validate_ident(name: str, value: str) -> None:
    83	    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
    84	        raise ValueError(
    85	            f"flow_subagent_dispatch: {name} must be a non-empty string "
    86	            f"(got {value!r}); refusing to interpolate into shell command"
    87	        )
    88	    if not _IDENT_RE.match(value):
    89	        raise ValueError(
    90	            f"flow_subagent_dispatch: {name}={value!r} contains characters "
    91	            f"outside the safe identifier set [A-Za-z0-9._+\\-]; refusing "
    92	            f"to interpolate into a shell=True command (R-class guard)"
    93	        )
    94	
    95	
    96	def _resolve_cmd_template() -> str:
    97	    """Resolve the dispatch command template.
    98	
    99	    Order:
   100	      1. ``FLOW_SUBAGENT_DISPATCH_CMD`` env var (highest priority - tests
   101	         + manual override + ops staging).
   102	      2. ``claude/capabilities/defaults.json`` ->
   103	         ``capabilities.autonomy_orchestrator.dispatch_cmd``.
   104	      3. ``RuntimeError`` - fail closed. The orchestrator's
   105	         ``_invoke_subagent_dispatch`` already raises a similar error if
   106	         the import fails; here we cover the "module imported but
   107	         neither config source supplies a template" case.
   108	    """
   109	    cmd = os.environ.get(ENV_VAR)
   110	    if cmd:
   111	        return cmd
   112	    if CAPABILITY_FILE.is_file():
   113	        try:
   114	            caps = json.loads(CAPABILITY_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   115	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):
   116	            # G/H-class: fall through to the explicit RuntimeError below
   117	            # rather than silently treating a corrupt capability file as
   118	            # "no config". Test-time isolation uses chdir-to-tmp so this
   119	            # path is normally unreachable in unit tests.
   120	            caps = None
   121	        if isinstance(caps, dict):
   122	            entry = (
   123	                caps.get("capabilities", {}).get("autonomy_orchestrator")
   124	                if isinstance(caps.get("capabilities"), dict)
   125	                else None
   126	            )
   127	            # Backward compat: some downstream consumers also accept a
   128	            # top-level entry (older v0.8.0 stub layout). We accept either.
   129	            if not entry and isinstance(caps.get("autonomy_orchestrator"), dict):
   130	                entry = caps["autonomy_orchestrator"]
   131	            if isinstance(entry, dict):
   132	                cmd = entry.get("dispatch_cmd")
   133	                if isinstance(cmd, str) and cmd:
   134	                    return cmd
   135	    raise RuntimeError(
   136	        f"subagent dispatch not configured: export {ENV_VAR} env var "
   137	        f"with the subagent invocation template. Template placeholders: "
   138	        f"{{slug}}, {{task_id}} (validated alnum+._+-); {{worktree}} "
   139	        f"(raw path, backward-compatible — wrap in your own shell quotes "
   140	        f"if needed) and {{worktree_quoted}} (shlex.quote()-wrapped, "
   141	        f"RECOMMENDED for shell=True). Example: "
   142	        f"'claude -p flow:flow-phase2-execute --slug {{slug}} "
   143	        f"--task {{task_id}} --worktree {{worktree_quoted}}'. "
   144	        f"v0.8.1 ships the dispatch shim infrastructure but the "
   145	        f"capability default (autonomy_orchestrator.dispatch_cmd) is "
   146	        f"intentionally absent — the SKILL handle is NOT a shell "
   147	        f"command. Production wire-up of the Claude CLI invocation "
   148	        f"template is v0.8.2 scope. v0.8.1 fails closed rather than "
   149	        f"silently skipping dispatch."
   150	    )
   151	
   152	
   153	def invoke(
   154	    ctx: Any,
   155	    *,
   156	    subagent_env: dict | None = None,
   157	    task_id: str | None = None,
   158	    **_kw: Any,
   159	) -> None:
   160	    """Called by orchestrator's ``_invoke_subagent_dispatch``.
   161	
   162	    ``ctx`` is duck-typed: ``ctx.worktree_path`` (Path or str) +
   163	    ``ctx.slug``. ``task_id`` is taken from the explicit kwarg first
   164	    (orchestrator passes ``manifest.id``); only as a last-resort fallback
   165	    do we look at ``ctx.task_id`` — production WorktreeContext does NOT
   166	    define ``task_id`` (it's only on the per-iteration manifest), so the
   167	    kwarg path is the canonical wiring. Falling back to ``getattr`` keeps
   168	    older test fixtures (which set ``ctx.task_id`` directly) working
   169	    while production uses the explicit kwarg path.
   170	
   171	    The subagent runs as a subprocess so its crash doesn't take down the
   172	    orchestrator. Nonzero return code is a *soft* signal - the orchestrator's
   173	    manifest verify (T11) and gate runner (T12) catch the empty/broken
   174	    diff and route appropriately. We only WARN here.
   175	    """
   176	    template = _resolve_cmd_template()
   177	
   178	    slug = getattr(ctx, "slug", "")
   179	    # F1 wiring: orchestrator-supplied task_id is authoritative; fall back
   180	    # to ctx attribute only for backward-compat with existing tests.
   181	    if task_id is None:
   182	        task_id = getattr(ctx, "task_id", "")
   183	    worktree_path = str(getattr(ctx, "worktree_path", ""))
   184	
   185	    _validate_ident("slug", slug)
   186	    # task_id is empty in some test scaffolding - allow that, but if
   187	    # non-empty validate the same way as slug.
   188	    if task_id:
   189	        _validate_ident("task_id", task_id)
   190	
   191	    # R-class hardening (codex round-1 F4 + round-2 P2): worktree path
   192	    # is NOT covered by ``_validate_ident`` (path may legitimately
   193	    # contain ``/`` and platform-specific chars). Two named placeholders
   194	    # to preserve operator-supplied quoting semantics:
   195	    #   {worktree}        — raw path; backward-compatible with templates
   196	    #                       that already wrap in shell quotes
   197	    #                       (e.g. ``--worktree "{worktree}"``).
   198	    #   {worktree_quoted} — shlex.quote()-wrapped; RECOMMENDED default
   199	    #                       for unquoted templates running under
   200	    #                       shell=True.
   201	    # Round-1 unconditionally substituted shlex.quote(...) into
   202	    # {worktree}, which broke quoted templates: outer quotes preserved
   203	    # the inner single-quotes literally and the subagent received a
   204	    # bogus path with quote chars.
   205	    cmd_str = template.format(
   206	        slug=slug,
   207	        task_id=task_id,
   208	        worktree=worktree_path,
   209	        worktree_quoted=shlex.quote(worktree_path),
   210	    )
   211	
   212	    # Merge in the orchestrator-supplied env (carries
   213	    # FLOW_AUTONOMY_PARENT_PID per T21/S5). subagent_env=None falls back
   214	    # to bare os.environ - tests that don't care about the parent-pid
   215	    # guard stay simple.
   216	    env = dict(os.environ)
   217	    if subagent_env:
   218	        env.update(subagent_env)
   219	
   220	    proc = subprocess.run(
   221	        cmd_str,
   222	        shell=True,
   223	        cwd=worktree_path or None,
   224	        env=env,
   225	        capture_output=False,
   226	        text=True,
   227	    )
   228	    if proc.returncode != 0:
   229	        print(
   230	            f"WARN: subagent dispatch returned {proc.returncode} for "

840	    # route to state 2 (auto_prepare_interrupted). DO NOT add a
   841	    # try/except that calls consume_auto_prepare_lock on failure.
   842	
   843	    ctx = create_task_worktree(
   844	        repo_root=repo_root,
   845	        slug=slug,
   846	        task_idx=task_idx,
   847	        integration_target=integration_target,
   848	    )
   849	    # §8.4 row `auto_engaged` requires 14 fields. Any missing key here
   850	    # causes `append_autonomy_event` to raise with the missing-field list
   851	    # — fail-loud BEFORE dispatch, which is the Q7.2 contract.
   852	    append_autonomy_event(
   853	        task_dir,
   854	        EVENT_AUTO_ENGAGED,
   855	        {
   856	            "event_id": _new_event_id(),
   857	            "ts": ctx.created_at,
   858	            "slug": slug,
   859	            "run_id": run_id,
   860	            "task_id": manifest.id,
   861	            "worktree_id": ctx.worktree_id,
   862	            "worktree_path": str(ctx.worktree_path),
   863	            "original_base_commit": ctx.original_base_commit,
   864	            "current_base_commit": ctx.current_base_commit,
   865	            "lifecycle_state": ctx.lifecycle_state,
   866	            "checkpoint_id": None,
   867	            "contract_path": str(contract_path),
   868	            "contract_hash": contract_hash,
   869	            "contract_schema_version": contract.contract_schema_version,
   870	        },
   871	    )
   872	    # [P2] Boundary marker durable on disk; consume the lock so state-3
   873	    # (post-auto_engaged crash) is the operative recovery path beyond
   874	    # this point. consume_auto_prepare_lock is os.replace + Y8 event;
   875	    # K-class: errors propagate so we never leave an inconsistent
   876	    # boundary (would manifest as orphan_lock_post_engaged on next run,
   877	    # which is recoverable but warrants a loud failure here).
   878	    consume_auto_prepare_lock(
   879	        task_dir, slug=slug, run_id=run_id, task_id=manifest.id,
   880	    )
   881	    # Subagent boundary — opaque. Return value INTENTIONALLY discarded
   882	    # (PRD §1.2: subagent narrative is advisory, never structured data).
   883	    #
   884	    # T21 / S5: propagate FLOW_AUTONOMY_PARENT_PID=<own pid> so that any
   885	    # `flow orchestrator --auto-execute` invocation the subagent attempts
   886	    # mechanically aborts via `_guard_against_nested_autonomy`. We pass a
   887	    # fresh copy of the environment (not a reference to os.environ) so
   888	    # downstream mutations cannot reach back into the parent process.
   889	    subagent_env = os.environ.copy()
   890	    subagent_env[AUTONOMY_PARENT_PID_ENV] = str(os.getpid())
   891	    # F1 wire-up (codex round-1): WorktreeContext intentionally has NO
   892	    # ``task_id`` field (the dataclass only carries worktree-level state;
   893	    # task identity lives on the per-iteration manifest). The dispatch
   894	    # shim's template needs ``{task_id}`` to invoke the subagent against
   895	    # the right task — pass it explicitly as a kwarg so the shim doesn't
   896	    # silently fall through ``getattr(ctx, "task_id", "")`` to "" and
   897	    # interpolate ``--task `` (empty arg). Test fixtures that set
   898	    # ``ctx.task_id`` directly still work (kwarg None → getattr fallback)
   899	    # but production now goes through the canonical path.
   900	    dispatch_fn(ctx, subagent_env=subagent_env, task_id=manifest.id)
   901	    # Authoritative facts come from disk, not the dispatch return value.
   902	    facts = derive_task_facts(ctx)
   903	
   904	    # T11 — manifest verification on actual diff (§1 row 3 / row 4).
   905	    verdict = verify_manifest_against_facts(contract, manifest, facts)
   906	    if verdict.decision == "block":
   907	        why = (
   908	            f"manifest_violation (row {verdict.block_row}): "
   909	            f"{verdict.violations}"
   910	        )
   911	        choices = [
   912	            "abort_task",
   913	            "switch_to_interactive",
   914	            "extend_scope_with_rationale",
   915	        ]
   916	        resume = f"flow orchestrator --resume {slug}"
   917	        if notifier is not None:
   918	            # T16 path — Notifier handles Tier 1 (blocked.md) + Tier 2
   919	            # (OSC 9 + BEL). Duck-typed: any object exposing
   920	            # `fire_block(...)` returning the blocked.md path works.
   921	            blocked_md_path = notifier.fire_block(
   922	                block_type="manifest_violation",
   923	                phase=2,
   924	                task_id=manifest.id,
   925	                issue_id="manifest_violation",
   926	                why_blocked=why,
   927	                required_choice=choices,
   928	                safe_resume_command=resume,
   929	                frontmatter_extra={"block_row": verdict.block_row},
   930	            )
  5860	      4. MergeRunner.merge_task() — gate 7 + R3 steps 1-7 (T14)
  5861	      5. Gate8VerificationRunner.verify() — gate 8 + 9a/9b (T15)
  5862	      6. MergeQueue.can_proceed() — S1 wave block (T15)
  5863	
  5864	    Exit codes (per the Flow global registry in
  5865	    ``scripts/common/exit_codes.py``; v0.8.2.1 migrated park 2 → 5):
  5866	      0 = all manifests merged + verified, OR contract missing →
  5867	          interactive fallback, OR pre-lock recovery → fail-closed
  5868	          interactive (legal silent — user never opted in this attempt).
  5869	      2 = USAGE_ERROR (reserved). Argparse / CLI misuse. Not produced
  5870	          by ``_cmd_auto_execute`` itself — kept distinct from park
  5871	          per v0.8.2.1 disambiguation; 5 internal CLIs (flow.py,
  5872	          flow_doctor.py, flow_promote.py, flow_autosave.py,
  5873	          flow_ralph.sh) emit rc=2 on argparse failure.
  5874	      3 = block raised at any phase (recovery / dispatch / gate 1-6 /
  5875	          merge / gate 8 / wave halt) OR Phase 2 terminal hard-stop
  5876	          (budget_hit / retry_cap / codex_review_cap / afk_aborted /
  5877	          afk_hard_cap — all write unified HardStopSnapshot v1).
  5878	          Distinct from exit 4 (S5 nested-autonomy aborted_nested).
  5879	      5 = AFK idle park (recoverable). Wait-mode AFK timeout without
  5880	          24h hard cap. No blocked.md, no snapshot, no merge. Operator
  5881	          runs /flow:resume to continue. Distinct from rc=3 terminal
  5882	          blocks. (v0.8.2.1: was rc=2 in v0.8.2; migrated to rc=5 =
  5883	          PARKED_RECOVERABLE.)
  5884	
  5885	    Staleness gate (T20) is OUT-OF-LOOP for v0.8.1 — operator runs
  5886	    `flow doctor <slug>` manually before invoking --auto-execute.
  5887	    v0.8.2 promotes to gate-0 in the dispatch loop.
  5888	
  5889	    AFK + Budget + retry-loop wired in v0.8.2 T1/T2/T3 + T3.1: Phase 2
  5890	    flows through `dispatch_with_retry` via `_phase2_dispatch`. T18
  5891	    extends the prod implementer-round adapter to actually re-dispatch
  5892	    on retry rounds (today: round-1 facts only, retries reuse them).
  5893	    """
  5894	    # Parse + R11 ceiling check via build_plan; raises SystemExit on
  5895	    # too-new schema before we touch the dispatch loop.
  5896	    plan = build_plan(slug)
  5897	    if plan.contract is None:
  5898	        # v0.8.0 fallback: contract missing/invalid → interactive.
  5899	        # Print to stderr so the test fixture can grep for the reason.
  5900	        print(
  5901	            f"INFO: {plan.fallback_reason} — running interactive instead.",
  5902	            file=sys.stderr,
  5903	        )
  5904	        return 0
  5905	
  5906	    task_dir = _resolve_slug_dir(slug)
  5907	    # task_dir = .flow/tasks/<slug>  →  parents[2] = repo root
  5908	    repo_root = task_dir.parents[2]
  5909	    contract_path = task_dir / "contract.json"
  5910	    contract_hash = hashlib.sha256(contract_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
  5911	    run_id = _resolve_or_create_run_id(task_dir)
  5912	    gate_cmds = _resolve_gate_commands(plan.contract)
  5913	    integration_target = _resolve_integration_target(plan.contract)
  5914	    criteria = list(plan.contract.acceptance_criteria or [])
  5915	    notifier = Notifier(
  5916	        contract=plan.contract, slug=slug, task_dir=task_dir,
  5917	    )
  5918	
  5919	    for task_idx, manifest in enumerate(plan.manifests):
  5920	        task_id = manifest.id
  5921	
  5922	        # ── [P1 codex round-1] skip already-completed tasks ────────────
  5923	        # CrashRecoveryDispatcher only classifies CRASH states; a journal
  5924	        # with a clean task_completed event for this (run_id, task_id)
  5925	        # is NOT a crash → classify returns clean / proceed. Without
  5926	        # this gate, rerunning ``--auto-execute`` against a
  5927	        # half-finished plan re-dispatches and re-merges already-merged
  5928	        # tasks. Per-task completion is the resume-correctness boundary;
  5929	        # we keep it OUTSIDE the dispatcher so the dispatcher's job
  5930	        # (crash-state classification) stays focused (separation of
  5931	        # concerns — sticking it inside classify() would mean future
  5932	        # readers conflate "no crash" with "no work to do").
  5933	        if _task_already_completed(
  5934	            task_dir, run_id=run_id, task_id=task_id,
  5935	        ):
  5936	            print(
  5937	                f"INFO: task {task_id} already completed for run "
  5938	                f"{run_id}; skipping.",
  5939	                file=sys.stderr,
  5940	            )
  5941	            continue
  5942	
  5943	        # ── Recovery gate (T19) ────────────────────────────────────────
  5944	        dispatcher = CrashRecoveryDispatcher(
  5945	            task_dir=task_dir,
  5360	
  5361	    Failure handling (codex round-1 D §1, G §1):
  5362	      * Worktree creation errors (subprocess.CalledProcessError, OSError)
  5363	        → wrapped in ``InfraFailureError`` so the caller can route to a
  5364	        terminal phase2_infra_failure block instead of bumping retry
  5365	        counters.
  5366	      * Subagent shim errors (RuntimeError from ``_invoke_subagent_dispatch``)
  5367	        → wrapped in ``InfraFailureError`` for the same reason. ``ctx``
  5368	        IS retained on the exception (as ``e.ctx`` attribute) so the
  5369	        caller can still record the orphan worktree for cleanup at task
  5370	        end (codex D §3 — orphan ctx must be recordable).
  5371	      * Fact derivation errors (subprocess.CalledProcessError from git)
  5372	        → wrapped in ``InfraFailureError`` with ``e.ctx`` set.
  5373	
  5374	    No partial state is exposed: if any step raises, the caller MUST NOT
  5375	    treat the helper's return value as valid (Python guarantees this —
  5376	    no return tuple is ever produced for the failing path).
  5377	    """
  5378	    if round_num < 2:
  5379	        raise ValueError(
  5380	            "_dispatch_implementer_fresh_worktree is for retry rounds "
  5381	            f"only (round_num >= 2); got round_num={round_num!r}"
  5382	        )
  5383	
  5384	    # Step 1: fresh worktree (round-discriminator naming).
  5385	    try:
  5386	        ctx = create_task_worktree(
  5387	            repo_root=repo_root,
  5388	            slug=slug,
  5389	            task_idx=task_idx,
  5390	            integration_target=integration_target,
  5391	            round_num=round_num,
  5392	        )
  5393	    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, OSError) as e:
  5394	        # No ctx exists yet — orphan-cleanup is not applicable.
  5395	        raise InfraFailureError(
  5396	            f"worktree creation failed for round {round_num}: {e}"
  5397	        ) from e
  5398	
  5399	    # Step 2: subagent dispatch. Wrap shim errors so caller can route
  5400	    # terminal. Same env-propagation pattern as auto_dispatch_task
  5401	    # (T21 / S5 nested-autonomy guard + AUTONOMY_PARENT_PID).
  5402	    subagent_env = os.environ.copy()
  5403	    subagent_env[AUTONOMY_PARENT_PID_ENV] = str(os.getpid())
  5404	    try:
  5405	        _invoke_subagent_dispatch(
  5406	            ctx,
  5407	            subagent_env=subagent_env,
  5408	            task_id=task_id,
  5409	            prompt_prefix=prompt_prefix,
  5410	        )
  5411	    except RuntimeError as e:
  5412	        # Attach ctx to the exception so the retry-loop caller can
  5413	        # forensic-cleanup the orphaned worktree at task end.
  5414	        infra = InfraFailureError(
  5415	            f"subagent dispatch failed for round {round_num}: {e}"
  5416	        )
  5417	        infra.ctx = ctx  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
  5418	        raise infra from e
  5419	
  5420	    # Step 3: derive authoritative facts from disk (G-class — never
  5421	    # trust subagent narrative; PRD §1.2). Wrap git failures so callers
  5422	    # don't see a bare CalledProcessError leak through the retry loop.
  5423	    try:
  5424	        facts = derive_task_facts(ctx)
  5425	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
  5426	        infra = InfraFailureError(
  5427	            f"fact derivation failed for round {round_num}: {e}"
  5428	        )
  5429	        infra.ctx = ctx  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
  5430	        raise infra from e
  5431	
  5432	    # Deltas: empty for now (parity with round-1 _prod_impl). Future
  5433	    # P0.x will wire token / cost capture from the subagent dispatch
  5434	    # return path. Empty dict is safe — `_apply_impl_deltas_to_budget`
  5435	    # handles {} as a no-op.

item.text:
**Verdict: YELLOW**. R1 fixes are mostly addressed in the plan, but one path typo is a P0 because it can implement the wrong, non-gitignored runtime location.

**P0**
- Path inconsistency: file-location AC says `<repo_root>/.flow/.runtime/...`, and `.gitignore` covers `.flow/.runtime/`, but the path-computation AC says `repo_root / ".flow" / "runtime" / ...`. That missing dot is a real footgun. Change it to `repo_root / ".flow" / ".runtime" / ...` everywhere, including docs/tests.

**P1**
- Repo-root derivation via `ctx.worktree_path.parents[2]` is acceptable for implementer worktrees created by `create_task_worktree` at `<repo_root>/.claude/worktrees/<id>`, confirmed by current shape in [scripts/flow_orchestrator.py](/data/Claude/flow-framework/scripts/flow_orchestrator.py:456). It is wrong for verify worktrees under `worktrees/verify/...`, but P0.2 dispatch does not use verify contexts. Add an explicit layout assertion/error in `invoke()` so future misuse fails loud.
- `_cmd_auto_execute` prefix-build before recovery classify is safe if `_render_task_brief` remains read-only. Current PRD text says “build prefix before auto_dispatch_task”; make explicit it must happen after `_task_already_completed`/recovery decision, immediately before Round 1 dispatch, unless the implementation already proves no side effects.
- `Formatter().parse()` is the right fix; cost is trivial for operator-controlled templates.

**Adversarial**
- A template can include `{prompt_prefix_file}` but not `cat` it into the model prompt. Your doc warning is sufficient; this is not mechanically enforceable without parsing shell semantics.
- New file transport surface is acceptable if prefix file is mode-default local forensic data. If prefix may contain sensitive review text, document retention/cleanup as intentional.

**AC Deltas**
- Add byte-for-byte content test: prefix file content equals `build_implementer_prompt(...)` output, UTF-8, no BOM/line-ending mutation.
- Add test that asserts the path specifically contains `.flow/.runtime`, catching the current typo.
- Existing 9-test plan otherwise covers the R1 P0/P1/adversarial findings.
